mechanism_narrative: Sustained increases in monthly real retail and food services sales (RSAFS) indicate robust consumer spending, which drives corporate revenue growth and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC of RSAFS momentum is negative over any 2-year rolling window, or positive forward returns occur in less than 55% of months when RSAFS rises by more than 0.5% month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in the unemployment rate above a critical threshold signals the onset of a recessionary contraction in labor demand, leading to deteriorating corporate earnings and negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: After the trigger fires, S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of instances, or the average forward return is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneously rising federal funds rates and falling housing starts indicate monetary tightening that constrains credit-sensitive sectors, curtailing economic growth and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: The interaction term (FEDFUNDS increase * HOUST decrease) fails to predict negative returns in >55% of triggered months, or its partial correlation with forward returns is not significantly negative over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: During the early recovery phase after a recession trough, strong nonfarm payroll gains signal self-sustaining economic momentum, which boosts corporate profits and drives positive S&P 500 returns over a 20-day horizon.
regime_hint: recession recovery phase within 12 months of NBER trough
failure_condition: In labeled recovery periods, months with PAYEMS increase above 200k do not produce positive average forward returns, or the signal is not significantly different from zero (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in industrial production (INDPRO) warns of a slowdown in manufacturing activity and aggregate supply, which precedes earnings deterioration and leads to negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: After a negative INDPRO print, average 60-day S&P 500 return is positive, or the signal-to-noise ratio for negative returns is below 0.3 over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate rises above a key threshold, it immediately increases the cost of capital and discount rates for equities, causing a short-term negative repricing in the S&P 500 over the following week.
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle, rate above neutral
failure_condition: Within 5 days after a rate crossing, the S&P 500 posts positive returns more than 50% of the time, or the average return is not significantly negative (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: When headline CPI inflation exceeds a moderate level, it erodes real purchasing power and forces the central bank to tighten policy, creating a persistent headwind for equity valuations and leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the month.
regime_hint: high inflation regime with year-over-year CPI > 3%
failure_condition: In months where year-over-year CPI exceeds 3%, average 20-day S&P 500 returns are not negative, or the correlation between CPI level and forward returns is not statistically significant at the 10% level.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneously rising retail sales and housing starts indicate a broad-based demand expansion that strengthens corporate revenues across sectors, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
regime_hint: simultaneous demand expansion across consumer and housing
failure_condition: When both RSAFS and HOUST increase month-over-month, the average 60-day S&P 500 return is not positive, or the joint signal does not outperform a simple single-series trend strategy over any 3-year period.
